mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction permits combined with stable short-term rates signal robust credit-driven demand expansion, which boosts financial sector profitability and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in more than 60% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression over a 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating industrial production, a concurrent deceleration in core inflation compresses real borrowing costs and validates profit margin expansion, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 1.0% month-over-month without a corresponding CPIAUCSL deceleration, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate demand, which directly increases corporate revenue streams and supports higher equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if quarterly GDP growth rates are positively correlated with negative 60-day forward SPX returns in more than 55% of observations, or if the rolling correlation coefficient falls below 0.10 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 additions in a single month, the labor market demonstrates sufficient strength to sustain consumer spending, triggering positive earnings revisions and lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered months, or the predictive rank correlation drops below 0.02 when tested against a rolling 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate combined with declining real estate sales volumes signals tightening financial conditions that restrict corporate leverage and dampen equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction effect becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero when controlling for concurrent changes in the UNRATE series.

mechanism_narrative: During supply-constrained environments where industrial production growth outpaces GDP growth, capacity utilization tightens and pricing power expands, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5% while GDPC1 growth remains below 1.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 45% in recessionary periods.
